name: Char Dham Yatra 9N/10D
title: Char Dham Yatra Package 9 Nights 10 Days from Haridwar 2026
meta_description: Char Dham Yatra 9N/10D from Haridwar starting Rs 19,800. All-inclusive: hotel, meals, private vehicle. Visit Yamunotri, Gangotri, Kedarnath, Badrinath. Book now.
keywords: char dham yatra 9 nights 10 days, char dham package from haridwar, kedarnath badrinath yamunotri gangotri tour, char dham 2026 booking
hero_tagline: The complete sacred journey to all four dhams — Yamunotri, Gangotri, Kedarnath, and Badrinath. Hotel, meals, and private vehicle from Haridwar included.
wa_text: Char Dham Yatra 9N/10D package details chahiye
overview:
The Char Dham Yatra is the most sacred Hindu pilgrimage in India, covering four ancient temples in Uttarakhand. Our 10-day all-inclusive package starts from Haridwar and takes you through scenic Himalayan valleys to Yamunotri (source of Yamuna), Gangotri (source of Ganga), Kedarnath (one of 12 Jyotirlingas dedicated to Lord Shiva), and Badrinath (one of four sacred dhams of Lord Vishnu).

Junegiri Yatra has been organising Char Dham journeys for 8+ years, with hotel arrangements at every stop, breakfast and dinner included, private vehicle for your group throughout, and trained drivers who know every mountain route. Senior citizens are welcome — we arrange palki and pony services for the Kedarnath trek and helicopter options on request.

itinerary.title: Haridwar to Barkot
itinerary.desc: Pickup from Haridwar in the morning. Drive to Barkot via Mussoorie, passing Kempty Falls. Beautiful pine forests and mountain roads. Arrive Barkot, check into hotel, dinner and rest.

itinerary.title: Yamunotri Yatra
itinerary.meta: 45 km drive + 6 km trek each way
itinerary.desc: Early morning drive to Jankichatti. Trek 6 km to Yamunotri Temple. Holy dip at Surya Kund. Darshan at the temple — source of Yamuna river. Return trek to Jankichatti, drive back to Barkot.

itinerary.title: Barkot to Uttarkashi
itinerary.desc: Scenic drive through pine forests to Uttarkashi. Visit Vishwanath Temple in Uttarkashi. Evening at leisure on the banks of Bhagirathi.

itinerary.title: Gangotri Yatra
itinerary.desc: Drive to Gangotri through Harsil Valley. Holy dip at Bhagirathi origin. Darshan at Gangotri Temple — source of holy Ganga. Return to Uttarkashi.

itinerary.title: Uttarkashi to Guptkashi
itinerary.desc: Long scenic drive across the Garhwal Himalayas. Pass through tea estates and apple orchards. Arrive Guptkashi by evening, check into hotel.

itinerary.title: Guptkashi to Kedarnath
itinerary.desc: Early morning drive to Sonprayag, take local jeep to Gaurikund. Begin trek to Kedarnath. Ponies, palkis, and helicopter available. Visit Kedarnath Temple — one of 12 Jyotirlingas. Attend evening Sandhya Aarti.

itinerary.title: Kedarnath to Guptkashi
itinerary.desc: Attend early morning aarti at Kedarnath Temple. Begin return trek to Gaurikund. Drive back to Guptkashi for overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Guptkashi to Badrinath
itinerary.desc: Drive to Pandukeshwar via Karna Prayag and Nandprayag (sacred river confluences). Arrive Pandukeshwar/Badrinath, evening check-in.

itinerary.title: Badrinath Darshan + Mana Village
itinerary.desc: Morning Badrinath Temple darshan. Holy dip at Tapt Kund (hot spring). Visit Brahma Kapal, Mana Village (last village of India), Vyas Gufa, Bheem Pul, and Saraswati River origin.

itinerary.title: Badrinath to Haridwar Drop
itinerary.desc: Morning drive back to Haridwar via Rishikesh. Brief stop for Ganga Aarti at Parmarth Niketan if time permits. Drop at Haridwar railway station or airport. Yatra complete with the blessings of all four dhams.

inclusions: Daily breakfast and dinner at hotels
Private non-AC vehicle for entire trip
Experienced driver familiar with Himalayan routes
All toll taxes, parking fees, driver allowance
Pickup and drop from Haridwar railway station/airport
exclusions: Airfare or train fare to Haridwar
Helicopter charges for Kedarnath (optional, available on request)
Pony, palki, or doli charges at Kedarnath/Yamunotri
VIP darshan, special poojas, Pind Daan
Any item not mentioned in inclusions

testimonials.quote: Did Char Dham with my parents last May. Junegiri team handled everything end-to-end. Hotels were clean, driver was respectful, and they even arranged a helicopter for my mother who couldn't trek. Highly recommend.
testimonials.author: Rajesh Kumar

testimonials.quote: First time doing Char Dham, was nervous about logistics. Junegiri made it stress-free. WhatsApp support was responsive throughout. The Mana Village stop was an unexpected highlight.
testimonials.author: Priya Sharma

testimonials.quote: Travelled as a group of 10 from Bangalore. Tempo Traveller was comfortable, food was good, and the price was fair. Will definitely book Do Dham next year.
testimonials.author: Anil Gupta

faq.q: What is the best time for Char Dham Yatra in 2026?
faq.a: Char Dham temple gates open from late April/early May (Akshay Tritiya) and close in November (Bhai Dooj). The best months are May, June, September, and October. July-August has heavy monsoon rains and landslide risks, so we advise against it. Most pilgrims prefer May or early June for the best weather and full darshan availability.

faq.q: How difficult is the Char Dham Yatra physically?
faq.a: The yatra involves three treks: Yamunotri (6 km one way), Kedarnath (19-22 km one way), and Hemkund (if added). Yamunotri is moderate. Kedarnath is challenging but manageable with palki, pony, or helicopter support. We help arrange all of these. Senior citizens and those with health issues are encouraged to take helicopter for Kedarnath.

faq.q: Can I do Char Dham Yatra by helicopter?
faq.a: Yes. Helicopter Char Dham Yatra is available — typically 5-6 days, costs Rs 1,80,000-2,10,000 per person. We can arrange this. For a hybrid, you can take helicopter only for Kedarnath while doing other dhams by road — this saves time and cost.

faq.q: Is registration required for Char Dham Yatra?
faq.a: Yes. The Uttarakhand government requires all yatris to register at registrationandtouristcare.uk.gov.in. We help you complete the registration. You'll receive a yatra registration certificate which you must carry.

faq.q: What is the total distance covered in this 10-day package?
faq.a: Approximately 1,750-1,800 km by road, plus 50 km of trekking (combined total for Yamunotri 12 km + Kedarnath 38-44 km return). Long days of mountain driving — comfortable vehicle and good rest at hotels are essential.

faq.q: What should I pack for Char Dham Yatra?
faq.a: Layered woollens, sweater and shawl (cold at high altitudes), sturdy trekking shoes, raincoat or umbrella, sunscreen, cap, sunglasses, personal medicines, power bank, ID proof, and a small day backpack. We share a detailed packing checklist after booking.

faq.q: How do I book and what is the advance payment?
faq.a: WhatsApp us at +91 98738 97652 with your preferred dates and group size. We send a detailed quote within 30 minutes. Confirm with 10-20% advance via UPI or bank transfer. Remaining payment is due before yatra start.

faq.q: Can I customize this itinerary?
faq.a: Absolutely. You can add Hemkund Sahib, Valley of Flowers, extra night in Rishikesh, helicopter Kedarnath, or shorten/extend the journey. Tell us your preferences and we'll build a custom plan.

name: Kedarnath Yatra 3N/4D
title: Kedarnath Yatra Package 3 Nights 4 Days from Haridwar 2026
meta_description: Kedarnath Yatra 3N/4D from Haridwar starting Rs 7,590. All-inclusive Jyotirlinga darshan package with hotel, meals, private vehicle, palki/pony arrangement. Book now.
keywords: kedarnath yatra package, kedarnath 3 nights 4 days, kedarnath from haridwar, kedarnath jyotirlinga tour 2026
hero_tagline: Sacred journey to Kedarnath — one of 12 Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva. All-inclusive package from Haridwar with hotel, meals, transport, and palki/pony arrangement on request.
overview:
Kedarnath, nestled at 3,583 metres in the Garhwal Himalayas, is one of the most revered Hindu pilgrimage sites in India. Home to one of the twelve Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva, the temple has stood for over 1,200 years against the harshest mountain weather. Our 4-day all-inclusive package from Haridwar makes this sacred journey accessible to everyone.

The package covers two nights in Guptkashi (a comfortable mountain town with good hotels), one night in Kedarnath itself, and includes the 19-22 km trek from Gaurikund. Pony, palki, and helicopter services are available on request. Senior citizens and first-time pilgrims welcome — our team handles every logistical detail.

itinerary.title: Haridwar to Guptkashi
itinerary.desc: Pickup from Haridwar in the morning. Scenic drive through Rishikesh, Devprayag (confluence of Alaknanda and Bhagirathi rivers), Rudraprayag, and Srinagar. En-route visit Dhari Devi Temple. Evening arrival at Guptkashi, check into hotel.

itinerary.title: Guptkashi to Kedarnath
itinerary.desc: Early morning drive to Sonprayag (30 km). Take local jeep to Gaurikund (5 km). Begin 22 km trek to Kedarnath. Ponies and palkis are available — Rs 3,000-5,000 one way. Helicopter from Phata or Sersi also available (Rs 7,000-8,500 one way). Reach Kedarnath, check into guesthouse, attend evening Sandhya Aarti at the temple.

itinerary.title: Kedarnath to Guptkashi
itinerary.desc: Attend Mangala Aarti at Kedarnath Temple at dawn. Begin return trek to Gaurikund. Reach Sonprayag, drive back to Guptkashi. Rest at hotel.

itinerary.title: Guptkashi to Haridwar Drop
itinerary.desc: Morning drive back to Haridwar via Rudraprayag and Rishikesh. Drop at Haridwar railway station or airport by evening. Yatra complete.

inclusions: Daily breakfast and dinner at hotels
Private non-AC vehicle for the entire trip
All toll taxes, parking, driver allowance
exclusions: Airfare or train fare to Haridwar
Pony, palki, or helicopter charges at Gaurikund/Kedarnath

testimonials.quote: Wanted to do Kedarnath darshan but was worried about the trek. Junegiri arranged a pony for me up and helicopter down. Best decision. The team explained everything in advance. Felt very supported.
testimonials.author: Sunita Verma

testimonials.quote: Did Kedarnath in 4 days flat — perfect for someone with limited leave. Hotels in Guptkashi were better than expected. Driver was patient and knew the route well.
testimonials.author: Vikram Singh

testimonials.quote: Group of 6 friends. We did the full trek both ways. Junegiri's package gave us flexibility on timing — no pressure. Sandhya Aarti at Kedarnath was unforgettable.
testimonials.author: Aman Joshi

faq.q: How long is the Kedarnath trek and how difficult is it?
faq.a: The trek is 19-22 km one way from Gaurikund to Kedarnath, ascending from 1,985 m to 3,583 m. It takes 6-9 hours one way for moderately fit walkers. The path is paved but steep in sections. Ponies (Rs 3,000-5,000) and palkis (Rs 6,000-9,000) are available. Helicopter from Phata/Sersi/Guptkashi takes 8-10 minutes (Rs 7,000-8,500 one way).

faq.q: Is helicopter available for Kedarnath?
faq.a: Yes. Three helipads operate: Phata, Sersi, and Guptkashi. One-way cost is Rs 7,000-8,500 per person. Two-way (same day return) Rs 14,000-17,000. Bookings open in February for the season — we handle this for you. Subject to weather conditions.

faq.q: When does Kedarnath Temple open and close?
faq.a: The temple opens around Akshay Tritiya in late April/early May and closes on Bhai Dooj in late October/early November. Exact dates vary each year. Best months: May, June, September, early October. Avoid July-August (heavy monsoon).

faq.q: Can senior citizens do Kedarnath Yatra?
faq.a: Yes, with the right arrangements. We recommend helicopter for ascent and pony/palki for descent, or all-helicopter. We've arranged Kedarnath darshan for travellers in their 70s and 80s. Medical clearance and an oxygen cylinder (available at Gaurikund) are wise precautions.

faq.q: What is the registration requirement for Kedarnath Yatra?
faq.a: All pilgrims must register on the Uttarakhand government portal: registrationandtouristcare.uk.gov.in. Carry the printed certificate. We help complete registration after booking.

faq.q: What clothes should I pack for Kedarnath?
faq.a: Even in May-June, Kedarnath is cold (5-15°C). Pack: thermal innerwear, fleece jacket, windproof jacket, woollen cap, gloves, raincoat or poncho, trekking shoes, sunglasses, sunscreen, basic medicines, power bank.

name: Do Dham Yatra 5N/6D
title: Do Dham Yatra Kedarnath Badrinath 5N/6D from Haridwar 2026
meta_description: Do Dham Yatra 5N/6D from Haridwar starting Rs 11,550. Kedarnath + Badrinath darshan with hotel, meals, private vehicle. All-inclusive package. Book now.
keywords: do dham yatra, kedarnath badrinath package, do dham 5 nights 6 days, do dham from haridwar 2026
hero_tagline: The two holiest dhams in 6 days — Kedarnath and Badrinath. All-inclusive from Haridwar with hotel, meals, private vehicle, and full Mana Village exploration.
wa_text: Do Dham Yatra 5N/6D package details chahiye
overview:
The Do Dham Yatra combines the two most spiritually significant dhams in Uttarakhand — Kedarnath (Lord Shiva's Jyotirlinga) and Badrinath (Lord Vishnu's sacred shrine). For pilgrims with limited time, this 6-day package is the perfect balance of completeness and pace.

The journey starts from Haridwar, includes the Kedarnath trek (palki/pony/helicopter available), drives through dramatic Garhwal mountain passes, and ends with Badrinath darshan and a visit to Mana Village — the last village of India before the Tibetan border. Includes Bheem Pul, Vyas Gufa, Saraswati River origin, and the holy Tapt Kund hot spring.

itinerary.title: Haridwar to Guptkashi
itinerary.desc: Drive from Haridwar to Guptkashi via Devprayag, Rudraprayag. Visit Dhari Devi Temple en-route. Evening check-in at Guptkashi.

itinerary.title: Guptkashi to Kedarnath
itinerary.desc: Drive to Sonprayag, jeep to Gaurikund. Trek to Kedarnath (palki/pony/helicopter available). Visit Kedarnath Temple. Evening Sandhya Aarti.

itinerary.title: Kedarnath to Guptkashi
itinerary.desc: Mangala Aarti at dawn. Return trek to Gaurikund. Drive to Guptkashi.

itinerary.title: Guptkashi to Pandukeshwar
itinerary.desc: Scenic drive via Karna Prayag and Nandprayag. Arrive Pandukeshwar by evening.

itinerary.title: Badrinath Darshan + Mana Village
itinerary.desc: Morning Badrinath Temple darshan. Holy dip at Tapt Kund. Visit Brahma Kapal, Mana Village (last Indian village), Vyas Gufa, Bheem Pul, Saraswati River origin.

itinerary.title: Pandukeshwar to Haridwar
itinerary.desc: Drive back to Haridwar. Brief stop at Rishikesh for Ganga Aarti or Ram Jhula if time permits. Drop at Haridwar.

inclusions: Daily breakfast and dinner at hotels
All toll taxes, parking, driver allowance
exclusions: Pony, palki, helicopter charges at Kedarnath

testimonials.quote: Wanted to do both dhams but didn't have 10 days. Do Dham 6-day package was perfect. Mana Village was a beautiful surprise. The team was professional throughout.
testimonials.author: Meera Iyer

testimonials.quote: Booked for parents and they came back happy. Hotel in Pandukeshwar had great Badrinath views. Kedarnath helicopter saved my mother's knees.
testimonials.author: Rohit Mehra

testimonials.quote: Group of 8 colleagues. Tempo traveller was comfortable for long drives. Dinner spreads were generous. Will recommend to office friends.
testimonials.author: Kavita Reddy

faq.q: What is the difference between Do Dham and Char Dham Yatra?
faq.a: Do Dham covers Kedarnath + Badrinath (the two most popular dhams). Char Dham adds Yamunotri and Gangotri. Do Dham takes 5-6 days; Char Dham needs 9-11 days. Many pilgrims do Do Dham first and Char Dham later.

faq.q: Can I do Do Dham by helicopter?
faq.a: Yes — helicopter Do Dham is available (typically 1-2 days). Costs Rs 80,000-1,20,000 per person depending on operator. We can arrange this. For a hybrid, take helicopter for Kedarnath only and drive to Badrinath.

faq.q: What is Mana Village famous for?
faq.a: Mana is the last Indian village before the Tibetan border. Famous for: Vyas Gufa (where Maharishi Vyas dictated the Mahabharata), Bheem Pul (a natural rock bridge over the Saraswati River), and the Saraswati River origin. It's a 3 km drive from Badrinath and worth half a day.

faq.q: Is Badrinath open in winter?
faq.a: No. Badrinath Temple closes for winter (typically late November) and re-opens in late April/May. The temple deity is moved to Pandukeshwar/Joshimath for winter worship. Best time: May-June, September-October.

faq.q: How is the road condition between Kedarnath and Badrinath?
faq.a: The route via Karna Prayag is in good condition for most of the year. Some sections are narrow mountain roads — driving requires care. Our drivers are experienced. Drive time: 6-7 hours from Guptkashi to Pandukeshwar.

name: Golden Triangle Tour 5N/6D
title: Golden Triangle Tour 5 Nights 6 Days Delhi Agra Jaipur 2026
meta_description: Golden Triangle Tour 5N/6D from Rs 18,500 — Delhi, Agra, Jaipur. Taj Mahal, Amber Fort, Hawa Mahal. AC vehicle, 4-star hotels, English speaking guide. Book now.
keywords: golden triangle tour package, delhi agra jaipur tour, taj mahal tour package, golden triangle 5 days 6 nights
hero_tagline: India's most iconic tour route — Mughal Delhi, the Taj Mahal in Agra, and royal Pink City Jaipur. 6 days of palaces, forts, and timeless history with English-speaking guide.
wa_text: Golden Triangle Tour 5N/6D package details chahiye
overview:
The Golden Triangle is India's most popular tour circuit and the perfect introduction to North Indian heritage, food, and culture. The triangle connects three cities — Delhi (the historic capital), Agra (home of the Taj Mahal), and Jaipur (the Pink City and gateway to Rajasthan).

Our 6-day all-inclusive package includes 4-star hotels, AC vehicle with English-speaking driver, English-speaking guide at all monuments, monument entry fees, and daily breakfast. Visit the Taj Mahal at sunrise, explore the marble inlay craft of Agra, ride an elephant up to Amber Fort in Jaipur, and shop in the colourful bazaars of Old Delhi. Perfect for first-time visitors to India.

itinerary.title: Arrival in Delhi
itinerary.desc: Welcome to India! Pickup from Delhi airport. Transfer to hotel. Evening at leisure or optional Old Delhi food walk. Welcome dinner with the team.

itinerary.title: Delhi Sightseeing
itinerary.desc: Morning Old Delhi tour: Jama Masjid, Red Fort, Chandni Chowk rickshaw ride, Raj Ghat (Gandhi memorial). Afternoon New Delhi: India Gate, Rashtrapati Bhavan drive-by, Qutub Minar (UNESCO), Humayun's Tomb (UNESCO). Evening at leisure.

itinerary.title: Delhi to Agra via Mathura/Vrindavan (optional)
itinerary.desc: Morning drive to Agra. En-route optional stop at Mathura/Vrindavan (birthplace of Krishna). Afternoon visit Agra Fort (UNESCO) and Mehtab Bagh for sunset Taj Mahal view across the Yamuna. Evening cultural show optional.

itinerary.title: Taj Mahal at Sunrise + Drive to Jaipur
itinerary.desc: Pre-dawn visit to the Taj Mahal at sunrise (most magical time). Breakfast at hotel. En-route to Jaipur visit Fatehpur Sikri (UNESCO) and Abhaneri stepwell. Evening arrival in Jaipur, check into Pink City heritage hotel.

itinerary.title: Jaipur Sightseeing
itinerary.desc: Morning visit to Amber Fort with elephant or jeep ride up. Afternoon city tour: City Palace, Jantar Mantar (UNESCO observatory), Hawa Mahal photo stop. Evening leisure or optional Chokhi Dhani village resort dinner with folk dance.

itinerary.title: Jaipur to Delhi Drop
itinerary.desc: Morning at leisure for shopping in Jaipur bazaars (gemstones, textiles, blue pottery). Drive back to Delhi. Drop at airport or hotel. Tour ends.

inclusions: Private AC vehicle (Sedan/SUV/Innova based on group)
All monument entry fees (Taj Mahal, Red Fort, Qutub, Amber Fort, City Palace)

testimonials.quote: Travelled from London with my husband. Junegiri arranged everything from airport pickup to Taj Mahal sunrise. Driver was professional and knew Delhi like the back of his hand. Hotels were comfortable and well-located.
testimonials.author: Sarah Mitchell

testimonials.quote: First trip to India. Was nervous about logistics. Junegiri's WhatsApp support was incredible — replies in minutes. The Taj Mahal at sunrise was unforgettable. Highly recommend for first-timers.
testimonials.author: Michael Chen

testimonials.quote: Family of 4 — kids loved the elephant ride at Amber Fort. Guide was patient with our questions about Mughal history. Great value compared to other operators we considered.
testimonials.author: Priyanka & Rajiv Patel

faq.q: What is the best time for the Golden Triangle Tour?
faq.a: October to March is the best time — pleasant temperatures (15-28°C), dry weather, ideal for sightseeing. Avoid April-June (very hot, 40°C+) and July-September (monsoon humidity). December and January can be foggy in Delhi mornings.

faq.q: Is the Taj Mahal closed on any day?
faq.a: Yes — the Taj Mahal is closed every Friday. We plan the itinerary to avoid visiting Agra on Fridays. The Taj is open from sunrise to sunset on all other days.

faq.q: Can I do Golden Triangle as a day trip?
faq.a: Same-day Taj Mahal day tours from Delhi are popular (by Gatimaan Express train or AC car). However, we recommend at least 4 days to properly experience all three cities. The 5N/6D package is ideal for a relaxed pace.

faq.q: Are English-speaking guides included?
faq.a: Yes. Our package includes a certified English-speaking guide at all major monuments. We can also arrange French, Spanish, German, Italian, or Japanese guides on request (small surcharge). Specify language at the time of booking.

faq.q: What kind of hotels do you provide?
faq.a: Standard tier: comfortable 3-star hotels (Hotel Radisson, Pride Plaza class). Deluxe tier: 4-star (Crowne Plaza, Le Meridien class). Luxury tier: 5-star palace hotels (Taj Group, ITC, Oberoi). Specify your preference at booking.

faq.q: Can I extend the Golden Triangle to include Rajasthan?
faq.a: Absolutely. Popular extensions: Jaipur → Pushkar → Udaipur → Jodhpur → Jaisalmer (10-14 day Rajasthan add-on). Or Delhi → Varanasi → Khajuraho. We design custom itineraries — share your interests.

faq.q: Is the Golden Triangle safe for solo female travellers?
faq.a: Yes, with our private guided tour format. Driver and guide are vetted, hotels are reputable, and we maintain WhatsApp contact throughout. Many of our solo female guests have had wonderful experiences.

faq.q: What is the dress code for monuments?
faq.a: Modest dress recommended (covered shoulders and knees). Some religious sites require head covering (we provide). At the Taj Mahal, no large bags or food items. Comfortable walking shoes essential.

related.name: Taj Mahal Day Tour from Delhi

name: Taj Mahal Day Tour from Delhi
title: Taj Mahal Day Tour from Delhi by Car or Train 2026
meta_description: Same-day Taj Mahal tour from Delhi by AC car or Gatimaan Express train. Rs 5,500 per person. Includes Taj Mahal entry, Agra Fort, lunch, English guide. Book now.
keywords: taj mahal day tour from delhi, same day taj mahal tour, taj mahal by train delhi, gatimaan express tour
hero_tagline: Visit one of the Seven Wonders of the World in a single day. Pickup from Delhi at dawn, see the Taj Mahal at sunrise, explore Agra Fort, return to Delhi by evening.
transport: Private AC Vehicle or Gatimaan Express Train
wa_text: Taj Mahal Day Tour from Delhi book karna hai
overview:
The Taj Mahal day tour from Delhi is the most efficient way to experience India's iconic monument when you have only one day to spare. Built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal, the Taj Mahal is one of the New Seven Wonders of the World and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

Our day tour gets you to Agra at sunrise — the most beautiful time to see the white marble glow in soft morning light — explores the equally impressive Agra Fort (also UNESCO), includes lunch at a heritage hotel, and brings you back to Delhi by evening. Choose between AC private car (4 hours each way) or the high-speed Gatimaan Express train (1 hour 40 minutes).

itinerary.title: Pre-dawn Delhi Pickup
itinerary.desc: Early pickup from Delhi hotel. Drive to Agra via Yamuna Expressway (3.5-4 hours). Tea/coffee stop en-route. Train option: depart Hazrat Nizamuddin 6:00 AM by Gatimaan Express, arrive Agra Cantt 7:50 AM.

itinerary.title: Taj Mahal Sunrise Visit
itinerary.desc: Enter the Taj Mahal complex. Walk through the iconic Charbagh Garden. Marvel at the white marble mausoleum and intricate pietra dura inlay work. Photograph from multiple angles. Guide explains Mughal history and architectural details.

itinerary.title: Breakfast / Brunch
itinerary.desc: Indian or continental breakfast at a heritage hotel near the Taj.

itinerary.title: Agra Fort Tour
itinerary.desc: Visit Agra Fort — the Mughal residence and seat of power for over 100 years. See the Diwan-i-Am, Diwan-i-Khas, Sheesh Mahal, and the room where Shah Jahan was imprisoned with a view of his Taj Mahal.

itinerary.title: Marble Inlay Workshop
itinerary.desc: Visit a marble inlay craft workshop where artisans demonstrate the same techniques used at the Taj Mahal. Optional shopping for souvenirs.

itinerary.title: Return to Delhi
itinerary.desc: Drive back to Delhi via Yamuna Expressway. Drop at hotel or airport. Tour ends. Train option: depart Agra 5:50 PM by Gatimaan Express, arrive Delhi 7:30 PM.

inclusions: Pickup and drop from Delhi hotel/airport
Private AC vehicle OR Gatimaan Express train tickets (your choice)
Taj Mahal and Agra Fort entry fees

testimonials.quote: Did the day tour from Delhi by car. Pickup was on time at 5 AM. Reaching Taj Mahal at 8:30 AM was perfect — fewer crowds and beautiful morning light. Guide was knowledgeable.
testimonials.author: Emma Williams

testimonials.quote: Took the Gatimaan Express both ways — fast, comfortable, and a fun experience itself. Got back to Delhi by 7:30 PM with the whole day in Agra. Junegiri team handled everything seamlessly.
testimonials.author: James O'Connor

testimonials.quote: Long day but absolutely worth it. The Taj Mahal exceeded expectations. Loved that the package included Agra Fort too. Driver was excellent.
testimonials.author: Anita Krishnan

faq.q: Is the Taj Mahal day tour from Delhi tiring?
faq.a: It's a long day (about 14 hours) but well-paced. The Yamuna Expressway makes the drive comfortable. Train option is faster — only 1.5 hours each way. Most travellers find it manageable, especially since you're at the Taj for several hours in the cooler morning.

faq.q: Should I take the car or train option?
faq.a: Car: more flexible, can stop where you want, includes lunch at heritage hotel. Train (Gatimaan Express): faster, more comfortable seating, executive chair car AC class. Trains can be sold out in peak season — book 2 weeks ahead. Most foreigners prefer the train.

faq.q: What time does the Taj Mahal open?
faq.a: The Taj Mahal opens 30 minutes before sunrise (around 6:00-6:30 AM depending on season) and closes 30 minutes after sunset (around 6:30-7:00 PM). It is closed on Fridays for prayers.

faq.q: Can I avoid the long lines at the Taj Mahal?
faq.a: Yes. We can arrange fast-track tickets (skip-the-line) for an additional Rs 500 per person. We also time the visit to early morning when crowds are smaller. Indian queue and foreign queue are separate — foreigners often have shorter waits.

faq.q: What is the entry fee for the Taj Mahal?
faq.a: For Indian citizens: Rs 50. For foreign tourists: Rs 1,100 (about $13 USD). Both include shoe covers and a bottle of water. Mausoleum entry (closest to the cenotaphs) is an additional Rs 200. All entry fees are included in our package.

faq.q: Can I visit Mathura/Vrindavan on the way?
faq.a: Yes, with the car option only. Adds about 2 hours to the day. Mathura is the birthplace of Lord Krishna. Tell us at booking time and we'll adjust the itinerary.
